page: Microsoft Azure D64s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, relying instead on Premium Storage capabilities for external disk attachments.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core capabilities and memory throughput. The server achieved a top-tier PassMark CPU score of 89,862.33 and a strong multi-core stress-ng CPU performance of 73,215.97. Single-core stress-ng performance is average at 2,269.0, though single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression reached top-tier levels at 36.1 GB/sec and 15.13 GB/sec respectively. Memory bandwidth is strong, with read-only small block cached speeds at 5,383.29 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 129.8 GB/sec.

The lack of local storage requires users to provision external Premium Storage, which introduces network-based storage latency tradeoffs. However, database operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a top-tier PassMark database score of 33,165.52 and a strong Redis SET throughput of 116.73 million operations per second. The hardware also supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, contributing to strong prompt processing speeds in LLM inference benchmarks. This profile is suited for database hosting, web serving, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine in the Dsv7 family, powered by a 64-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and integrated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show top-tier PassMark CPU and database operation scores, alongside strong multi-core stress-ng results and high memory bandwidth. While single-core performance is average, the inclusion of AVX-512 and AMX instructions enables strong prompt processing speeds for small to large language models. Lacking local disks, the instance relies on external Premium Storage. This configuration is suited for database operations, multi-threaded enterprise applications, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v7 is a 64-vCPU general-purpose virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon processor, 256 GB of RAM, and strong multi-core performance suited for database operations and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v7 is a 64-vCPU virtual machine with 256 GB RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor for database and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced sixty-four vCPU virtual machine featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processing and strong multi-core throughput for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB RAM powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor
Top-tier PassMark CPU and database operation benchmark scores
Strong multi-threaded performance and high-bandwidth memory architecture
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage attachment
Supports advanced vector and matrix extensions including AVX-512 and AMX
Suited for database hosting, web serving, and multi-threaded enterprise applications
